_comment: A small, curated slice of the MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise knowledge base.
The full knowledge base is openly published by MITRE as STIX 2.1 JSON
(https://github.com/mitre/cti). We bundle a hand-picked subset here so the
pipeline works fully offline; mitre.py can also fetch the live STIX bundle
and fall back to this file when the network is unavailable.
Each technique records which LANL log signal (a knowledge-graph relation
produced by pipeline.build) it can be detected through, which is what lets
us link the ATT&CK knowledge base to the log-derived graph (LO4/LO7).

techniques.name: Valid Accounts
techniques.description: Adversaries obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts to gain access.

techniques.name: Remote Services
techniques.description: Adversaries use valid accounts to log into a service that accepts remote connections.

techniques.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries use RDP to move laterally between hosts.

techniques.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
techniques.description: Adversaries use SMB and administrative shares to move laterally.

techniques.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
techniques.description: Adversaries abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands.

techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
techniques.description: Adversaries communicate using application layer protocols such as DNS to blend in with normal traffic.
